data.title: Bybit Reports Theft Incident And Collaborates With Authorities
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on February 22, Bybit announced on social media that it has reported a recent theft incident to the relevant authorities. The company assured that any further information will be promptly communicated. Bybit has also engaged in swift and extensive collaboration with on-chain analysis providers to identify all involved addresses. These efforts aim to reduce and combat the ability of malicious actors to dispose of and sell ETH through legitimate markets, thereby narrowing the available channels for disposal.

data.title: Bybit: Has cooperated with on-chain analysis agencies to limit hackers from dumping ETH
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Bybit officially posted on the X platform that it has reported the theft to the relevant authorities and will update as soon as more information is available. Fortunately, we have quickly and extensively cooperated with on-chain analysis agencies to identify the relevant addresses involved. The relevant actions will mitigate and counter the ability of bad actors to dispose of and dump ETH in the market, and narrow the channels through which stolen funds can be disposed.

data.title: OpenSea CEO: The US SEC is about to end its investigation into OpenSea, and classifying NFTs as securities is a distortion of the law
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: OpenSea co-founder and CEO dfinzer posted on the X platform, "The U.S. SEC is about to end its investigation into OpenSea. This is a victory for every creator and builder in this field. Trying to classify NFTs as securities would be a step backwards - misinterpreting the law and hindering innovation."

data.title: Bloomberg: US SEC withdraws lawsuit against Coinbase, which has paid $50 million in legal fees
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Paul Grewal, Chief Legal Officer of Coinbase, in an interview, the US SEC has agreed to withdraw its lawsuit and not to bring it again. Coinbase has paid $50 million in legal fees for the case. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Security company Hacken CEO: Bybit reserves will be audited every two weeks, Bybit reserves are sufficient
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to Dyma Budorin, co-founder and CEO of Web3 security audit company Hacken Club, he posted on the X platform, "Hacken will follow up and audit Bybit's proof of reserves every two weeks. For details, see https://audits.hacken.io/bybit/. Once Bybit officially approves it, it will issue proof of assets exceeding liabilities. In addition, I am personally sure that Bybit has enough funds to pay off its debts and resolve this incident." The tweet was forwarded and confirmed by Bybit co-founder and CEO Ben Zhou. 
Subsequently, Hacken officially released an updated audit report on Bybit's proof of reserves.

data.title: Binance and Bitget have deposited more than 50,000 ETH into Bybit cold wallet
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to on-chain data, Binance and Bitget have deposited more than 50,000 ETH into Bybit's cold wallet address. Among them, Bitget transferred about 40,000 ETH.

data.title: Bybit official announcement: Over $1.5 billion in assets were stolen due to ETH multi-signature wallet transfer attack, reserve funds are strong, platform withdrawals and other services are operating normally
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News In response to the previous asset theft, Bybit officially released a detailed announcement of the incident for the first time: At 20:30 on February 21, Beijing time, Bybit detected unauthorized activity in the Ethereum cold wallet during a routine transfer. This transfer was part of Bybit's official plan to transfer ETH from the ETH multi-signature cold wallet to the hot wallet. Unfortunately, the transaction was manipulated by a complex attack that changed the smart contract logic and hid the signature interface, allowing the attacker to control the ETH cold wallet. As a result, more than 400,000 ETH and stETH with a total asset value of more than US$1.5 billion were transferred to an unknown address.
Stolen funds: ETH and stETH worth more than US$1.5 billion.
Main reason: During the planned routine transfer process, the ETH multi-signature cold wallet was maliciously manipulated by the transfer process.
Bybit reiterates the following key points: All other cold wallets under Bybit are safe and customer funds are not affected. Please be wary of other scams; although there has been a surge in withdrawal requests, excessive requests may cause withdrawal delays, but all withdrawals are being processed normally, and 70% of pending requests have been processed; Bybit's reserves are strong and 1:1 backed, and all customer assets are fully protected. Users can view relevant information on the Proof of Reserve (PoR) webpage.
At the same time, Bybit is working with leading blockchain forensics experts to track the stolen funds and resolve the situation; its security team is investigating the root cause, with special attention to potential vulnerabilities in the Safe.global platform user interface, which may be exploited during transactions. Bybit has an asset management scale of more than US$20 billion and will use bridge loans if necessary to ensure the availability of user funds. The Bybit platform and all other services, including trading products, cards and P2P, are operating normally.
